question: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by April 15, 2026?
narrative_context: Iran rejected Trump's 15-point peace plan on March 25, demanding Strait of Hormuz sovereignty and reparations (CNBC, March 25 2026)
Same day Iran rejected plan, it escalated attacks on Israel and Gulf states (Euronews, March 25 2026)
Market requires 14 consecutive zero-action days starting by April 1 with only 14 days until close (April 15); with both sides escalating, timeline is nearly impossible
Trump's envoy Witkoff presented framework; Iran rejected it. Active military operations continue as of April 1 (Al Jazeera, April 1 2026)
thesis: Market at 29.5% implies ~30% chance for sustained peace by April 15. Reality: ~10%. Iran publicly rejected negotiations, both sides are escalating, and the resolution mechanism requires 14 straight zero-action days starting from an escalating military situation. Even if talks restarted today, the timeline is impossibly tight.
x_thread: Polymarket: Iran-Israel/US conflict ends by April 15? 29.5%. Our estimate: 10%. Market is overpricing peace by 2-3x.
Iran rejected Trump's 15-point plan on March 25. Same day, escalated attacks. No peace talks have actually occurred.
Market resolution: requires 14 consecutive zero-military-action days starting by April 1. Current state: active airstrikes. Mathematically impossible on this timeline.
Don't chase the 'hope premium.' Ground truth: escalation, not de-escalation. Market @ 29% is pricing in miracles. Reality check: https://dub.sh/pb-x
